meta_title: Impact Doors in Kings Point, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact doors in Kings Point, FL. Protect your home from 170 mph winds. Licensed, A+ BBB, financing from $87/mo.
h1: Impact Doors for Kings Point Homes: Real Protection Where It Counts
intro:
Kings Point is one of Palm Beach County's most beloved active-adult communities, home to thousands of residents who have built comfortable, secure lives in the Kings Point Condominiums and the surrounding neighborhoods near Boynton Beach and Delray Beach. But comfort and security don't come automatically in South Florida — they have to be built in. With a rated wind zone of 170 mph and a storm history that includes direct hits from Hurricane Frances, Hurricane Wilma, and Hurricane Irma, Kings Point homeowners know better than most that the difference between a good season and a devastating one often comes down to the strength of your doors.

Approximately 70% of homes in the Kings Point area were built before 1994 — before the sweeping reforms to Florida's building codes that followed Hurricane Andrew. That means a significant portion of the community's entry doors, sliding glass doors, and French doors were installed before modern hurricane-impact standards were established. These older doors may look fine from the outside, but they can fail catastrophically under the pressure of a major storm, allowing wind, rain, and debris to compromise the structural integrity of your entire home. Upgrading to certified impact doors is not just a smart investment for a home valued around $220,000 — it's a layer of protection that safeguards everything inside those walls.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, known throughout Palm Beach County as The Kings of Service, we specialize in bringing Kings Point homes into full compliance with current Florida Building Code requirements while dramatically improving comfort, energy efficiency, and curb appeal. Whether you're protecting a ground-floor condo unit near the Kings Point Clubhouse or a single-family home just blocks from the Kings Point Theater, our team has the local knowledge, certified products, and in-house installation expertise to get the job done right — the first time.

section_why.h2: Why Kings Point Residents Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you live in a close-knit community like Kings Point, reputation travels fast. Neighbors talk, HOA boards compare notes, and word spreads quickly about which contractors deliver on their promises and which ones disappear after the deposit clears. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing has built its reputation across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties not through flashy advertising, but through consistent, high-quality work and a genuine commitment to the homeowners we serve. With over 500 homes completed and 60+ years of combined installation experience among our team, we bring a level of expertise to every Kings Point project that simply cannot be matched by out-of-area contractors or big-box installation crews.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract your project to a third party. The same trained, background-checked, and licensed technicians who represent our company are the ones who show up at your door, handle your permits with the Palm Beach County Building Division, and complete your installation from start to finish. This matters enormously in a community like Kings Point, where many residents are retired and want predictability, professionalism, and clear communication — not a revolving door of unfamiliar faces.

We also understand that Kings Point has unique logistical considerations. Many units in the Kings Point Condominiums have HOA approval processes, specific aesthetic requirements, and shared-wall situations that require careful coordination. Our team has extensive experience working within these frameworks, navigating HOA guidelines, condo association requirements, and building management schedules so that your installation goes smoothly without disrupting your neighbors or your daily routine. Our A+ BBB rating reflects not just the quality of our products, but the quality of every interaction we have with homeowners from initial consultation through final inspection.

From the moment you contact us for a free estimate to the day we pull your final permit and hand you your warranty documentation, Premier Impact Windows & Roofing is your single point of accountability. That's what it means to be The Kings of Service — and it's why Kings Point homeowners continue to refer us to their friends and family throughout the community.

section_history.h2: What Kings Point's Storm History Tells Us About Door Protection
section_history.body:
Kings Point has been tested by South Florida's storms more times than any homeowner would prefer. Since 1990, the area has experienced at least 7 significant storm events, three of which left lasting impressions on the community. Hurricane Frances made landfall in September 2004 as a Category 2 storm, bringing sustained winds and prolonged rainfall that exposed weaknesses in older door frames and weather seals throughout Palm Beach County. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma tore through the region in October 2005 as one of the most intense Atlantic hurricanes ever recorded, generating powerful wind shear and debris fields that shattered unprotected glass doors and buckled standard entry door frames across the county. Then came Hurricane Irma in 2017, a storm that stretched its destructive reach far inland, reminding residents of western Boynton Beach and the Kings Point area that distance from the coast does not equal safety from hurricane-force winds.

What these storms consistently revealed is a pattern that building scientists and insurance adjusters know well: when doors fail, homes fail. A compromised entry door or a sliding glass door that blows inward under wind pressure creates an internal pressurization event that can lift roofs, collapse walls, and turn a survivable storm into a total loss. For the many pre-1994 homes in Kings Point, the risk is especially acute. Older door assemblies lack the multi-point locking systems, reinforced frames, and impact-resistant glazing that modern hurricane doors provide. Upgrading those points of vulnerability before the next storm arrives is not pessimism — it's preparation rooted in hard-earned local experience.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Doors in Kings Point and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Kings Point falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction, which means all door replacements and upgrades must comply with the Florida Building Code (FBC) and be permitted through the Palm Beach County Building Division. While Kings Point is not located within the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies primarily to Miami-Dade and Broward coastal areas — that does not reduce the code requirements here. Palm Beach County sits within a 170 mph wind zone, and all impact door products installed in the area must be rated to meet or exceed that wind speed threshold per FBC guidelines.

This distinction matters when you're shopping for impact doors. Products that are approved for lower wind zones or that carry only Miami-Dade NOA (Notice of Acceptance) without broader FBC compliance may not be appropriate or legally installable in your Kings Point home. Every impact door we install at Premier Impact Windows & Roofing carries proper product approval documentation, and we handle the full permitting process with the Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf. This includes submitting product specifications, energy calculations where required, and scheduling inspections to ensure your installation receives a clean certificate of completion.

For residents of Kings Point Condominiums, there is an additional layer of consideration: condo associations often require proof of code compliance and HOA-approved finishes before installation begins. Our team is well-versed in managing these dual-approval processes, ensuring your new impact doors satisfy both the county's structural requirements and your association's aesthetic standards. When the project is complete, you'll have full documentation for your records, your insurance carrier, and any future buyer of your home.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Doors: Built for Kings Point's 170 MPH Wind Zone
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs impact doors from two of the industry's most trusted manufacturers: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are purpose-built for the demanding requirements of South Florida's storm season, and both carry the product approvals necessary for installation in Palm Beach County's 170 mph wind zone. Whether you're replacing a worn entry door, upgrading aging sliding glass doors, or installing elegant French doors in your Kings Point home, ESW and CWI offer configurations that combine uncompromising protection with beautiful, modern aesthetics.

ESW impact doors are engineered with heavy-duty aluminum frames, impact-laminated glass, and multi-point locking systems that engage at multiple points along the door frame simultaneously — not just at the handle. This distributed locking mechanism dramatically reduces the risk of door failure under the lateral pressure of hurricane-force winds. CWI's product line complements this with an exceptional range of sliding glass door and French door options that meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements while offering clean sight lines, smooth operation, and superior thermal performance. Both brands deliver impact-resistant glazing that doesn't just resist shattering — it holds together as a bonded unit even when struck by wind-driven debris at high speed.

For the Kings Point community, where many homeowners are looking to balance strong protection with low maintenance and visual appeal, these products represent an ideal solution. Impact doors from ESW and CWI require no hurricane shutters, no seasonal preparation, and no manual operation during storm warnings — your protection is built in, every day of the year. They also provide excellent sound attenuation, reducing noise from neighboring units and common areas, which is an underappreciated quality-of-life benefit in a condominium setting.

section_cost.h2: Impact Door Costs and Financing Options for Kings Point Homeowners
section_cost.body:
For a home valued around $220,000 in Kings Point, investing in impact doors is one of the highest-return upgrades you can make. The cost of impact door installation varies based on the number of openings, door type, size, and configuration, but most Kings Point homeowners can expect to invest anywhere from $1,500 to $4,500 per door opening for a professionally installed, code-compliant impact door system. Sliding glass door replacements and French door configurations may sit at the higher end of that range due to their larger glass surface areas and more complex framing requirements. Entry door replacements are often more straightforward and can be quite competitive in price.

At Premier Impact Windows & Roofing, we believe that protecting your home should not require depleting your savings. That's why we offer flexible financing options starting at just $87 per month, with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. Our financing programs are designed to make impact door upgrades accessible for middle-income homeowners throughout Kings Point without the financial stress of a large upfront payment. Many of our customers find that the monthly financing cost is partially or fully offset by the insurance savings they begin receiving after installation — making the upgrade feel even more financially sensible.

From a return-on-investment perspective, impact doors deliver value on multiple fronts. They increase resale value, particularly important in a community like Kings Point where buyers actively seek move-in-ready, code-compliant properties. They reduce energy bills by improving the thermal envelope of your home and blocking heat transfer through the door assembly. And they eliminate the cost and hassle of purchasing, storing, and installing hurricane shutters year after year. When you add it all up, the financial case for impact doors in Kings Point is compelling — and our team is happy to walk you through the numbers during your free in-home estimate.

section_insurance.h2: How Impact Doors Can Lower Your Homeowners Insurance in Kings Point
section_insurance.body:
Florida homeowners insurance has become one of the most significant ongoing costs for Palm Beach County residents, and Kings Point is no exception. Carriers operating in the state are required to offer discounts for homes with documented wind mitigation features — and impact doors are among the most recognized and rewarded upgrades you can make. After your new impact doors are installed and inspected, we recommend scheduling a wind mitigation inspection with a licensed inspector. The resulting report documents your upgraded protection to your insurance carrier and typically unlocks meaningful premium reductions.

The specific savings vary by carrier and policy structure, but Palm Beach County homeowners frequently report reductions in their wind premium of 15% to 35% after completing impact door and window upgrades. For a homeowner paying $4,000 to $6,000 annually in homeowners insurance — a common range in today's market — that translates to real money back in your pocket every year. Major carriers operating in South Florida, including Citizens Property Insurance and private market alternatives, all recognize wind mitigation credits for impact-rated openings that meet Florida Building Code standards.

It's also worth noting that impact doors contribute to your home's overall Opening Protection classification on a wind mitigation report. Achieving full opening protection — meaning all windows and doors are impact-rated — typically yields the highest available discount tier. If you've already upgraded your windows to impact-resistant units, adding impact doors completes that protection profile and may push you into a higher savings bracket. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing will provide you with all the product approval documentation and installation records you need to submit to your insurance carrier, making the claims and discount process as smooth as possible.

faqs.q: Do I need special approval from my Kings Point condo association before installing impact doors?
faqs.a: Yes, most Kings Point Condominiums have HOA or condo association approval requirements before any exterior modifications, including door replacements. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing works directly with associations and property managers to ensure all proposed door styles, finishes, and frame colors meet your community's guidelines before installation begins. We handle the documentation and coordination so you don't have to navigate the process alone.

faqs.q: How much does impact door installation typically cost in Kings Point, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: Impact door installation in Kings Point generally ranges from $1,500 to $4,500 per opening depending on door type, size, and complexity. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting at $87 per month with $0 down options for qualified homeowners, making it easy to upgrade without a large upfront payment. Contact us for a free, no-obligation estimate tailored to your specific home and door configuration.

faqs.q: Do impact door installations in Kings Point require a permit from Palm Beach County?
faqs.a: Yes, all impact door replacements in Kings Point require a permit issued through the Palm Beach County Building Division. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing manages the entire permitting process on your behalf, from submitting product approval documentation to scheduling county inspections. When the job is complete, you'll receive a finalized permit and inspection record for your files, your insurance carrier, and any future real estate transaction.

faqs.q: How long does it take to have impact doors installed in a Kings Point home?
faqs.a: The typical timeline from signed contract to completed installation is 4 to 8 weeks, which accounts for product ordering, permit processing with Palm Beach County, and scheduling. The physical installation of most impact doors is completed in a single day. Our team communicates clearly throughout the process and coordinates with your schedule — including any condo building management requirements — to minimize disruption.

faqs.q: Will installing impact doors actually lower my homeowners insurance premium in Kings Point?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. After installation, a licensed wind mitigation inspector can document your new impact doors and submit a report to your insurance carrier. Palm Beach County homeowners commonly see wind premium reductions of 15% to 35% after upgrading to impact-rated openings. If your windows are also impact-rated, completing your doors achieves full opening protection — the highest discount tier available under Florida's wind mitigation credit system.
